key_insight: RL post-training gains are highly layer-localized: training just one transformer layer can recover most, and sometimes more than, full-parameter RL improvement, with the strongest effects concentrated in middle layers.
engineering_relevance: For efficient LLM post-training, this suggests a low-cost way to target the most impactful transformer layer(s) instead of updating all parameters, reducing compute and potentially simplifying RL adaptation workflows.
method: Defined "layer contribution" as the fraction of full RL improvement recovered by training a single layer in isolation.
Evaluated layer-wise RL training across seven models from Qwen3 and Qwen2.5 using GRPO, GiGPO, and Dr. GRPO.
Compared rankings across mathematical reasoning, code generation, and agentic decision-making tasks to test stability.
evidence_signal: Across seven models, three RL algorithms, and multiple tasks, the abstract reports a remarkably stable pattern: a small subset of layers, often a single middle-layer, accounts for most of the RL gain.
watch_item: The result is based on layer-wise isolation studies, so practical deployment should verify whether the best layer choice holds under different training budgets, data distributions, and downstream objectives.
paper_title: Is One Layer Enough? Training A Single Transformer Layer Can Match Full-Parameter RL Training
